rationale: The citing paper explicitly states it “extends prior fluency and metacognition research” and identifies a gap in prior fluency work about ease leading to favorable evaluations, which directly aligns with the cited paper’s core findings on conceptual/perceptual fluency effects on brand evaluation. The cited paper appears to be a likely theory/mechanism source for the citing paper’s repositioning of fluency effects in established-brand contexts. Given this conceptual overlap, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
